name: Dioon
description: [FILL IN] Dioon is a genus of cycads in the family Zamiaceae, native to Mexico and Central America. Species are typically found in dry to semi-arid mountainous habitats and are among the most drought-tolerant of all cycads. The genus is notable for producing some of the largest and heaviest cycad cones known, and individual plants can live for over a thousand years.
etymology: [FILL IN] From the Greek 'di' (two) and 'oon' (egg), referring to the paired ovules on each megasporophyll.

conservation_status.notes: [FILL IN] Most species are threatened; several are Endangered or Critically Endangered due to over-collection and habitat loss.

distribution.habitat: [FILL IN] Dry to semi-arid tropical forests, oak-pine forests, rocky limestone hillsides.

morphology.stem: [FILL IN] Erect, stout, armoured cylindrical trunk.
morphology.leaves: [FILL IN] Pinnately compound; leaflets stiff and spine-tipped; bluish-green or grey-green in many species.
morphology.reproductive_structures: [FILL IN] Dioecious; produces very large cones — female cones among the largest of any cycad, weighing up to 20 kg in D. spinulosum.
morphology.seeds_or_spores: [FILL IN] Large seeds with hard, cream-coloured to white sarcotesta.
morphology.roots: [FILL IN] Coralloid roots with nitrogen-fixing cyanobacteria.

ecology.pollination: [FILL IN] Insect-pollinated (weevils recorded in some species).
ecology.seed_dispersal: [FILL IN] Vertebrate-dispersed; large mammals and birds may consume seeds.
ecology.symbioses: [FILL IN] Nitrogen-fixing cyanobacteria in coralloid roots.

uses.cultural: [FILL IN] Seeds used in traditional ceremonies and as food sources by local communities.
uses.food: [FILL IN] Seeds (tortillas) processed to remove cycasin toxins; used in some indigenous Mexican communities.
uses.horticultural: [FILL IN] Prized by collectors; extremely slow growing and long-lived.

cultivation.soil: [FILL IN] Well-drained, gritty or sandy soil; tolerates alkaline substrates.
cultivation.notes: [FILL IN] CITES Appendix I or II; some of the longest-lived plants in the world.

notable_species.notes: [FILL IN] Produces the world's heaviest cycad cones.

external_links.label: Dioon — Plants of the World Online
